name: Set Up Redis as Application Cache with TTL
description: Configure Redis as a fast in-memory cache for your application with key expiration policies to manage memory efficiently.
trendingReason: Caching is the most common Redis use case and a critical skill for reducing database load in production

steps.title: Verify Redis is running
steps.description: Confirm that your Redis server is up and responding to commands.
steps.tip: You should see PONG as the response. If not, start Redis with 'redis-server' or 'sudo systemctl start redis'.

steps.title: Set a key with a TTL
steps.description: Store a cached value with an automatic expiration time in seconds.
steps.tip: EX sets expiration in seconds. Use PX for milliseconds.

steps.title: Check remaining TTL on a key
steps.description: Inspect how many seconds remain before a key expires.

steps.title: Set a default eviction policy
steps.description: Configure Redis to automatically evict least-recently-used keys when memory is full.
steps.warning: This changes the running config only. Add 'maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru' to redis.conf for persistence across restarts.

steps.title: Set a memory limit
steps.description: Cap Redis memory usage to prevent it from consuming all available RAM.

steps.title: Verify cache hit and miss with GET
steps.description: Retrieve a cached value and confirm it returns data before expiration and nil after.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Cache Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Verifying Redis ==="
redis-cli ping

echo ""
echo "=== Configuring memory and eviction ==="
redis-cli CONFIG SET maxmemory 256mb
redis-cli CONFIG SET maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

echo ""
echo "=== Setting sample cached keys ==="
redis-cli SET user:1001:profile '{"name":"alice","email":"alice@example.com"}' EX 3600
redis-cli SET user:1002:profile '{"name":"bob","email":"bob@example.com"}' EX 3600
redis-cli SET api:rates:usd '{"rate":1.0}' EX 300

echo ""
echo "=== Verifying keys ==="
redis-cli TTL user:1001:profile
redis-cli GET user:1001:profile

echo ""
echo "=== Memory usage ==="
redis-cli INFO memory | grep used_memory_human

faq.question: What happens when a key expires?
faq.answer: Redis uses a combination of lazy expiration (checked on access) and periodic active expiration (sampling keys every 100ms). Expired keys are removed automatically and GET returns nil.

faq.question: Should I use allkeys-lru or volatile-lru?
faq.answer: Use allkeys-lru if all keys are cache data that can be regenerated. Use volatile-lru if you mix cached keys (with TTL) and persistent keys (without TTL) and only want to evict the cached ones.

faq.question: How do I check cache hit rate?
faq.answer: Run 'redis-cli INFO stats' and look at keyspace_hits and keyspace_misses. Hit rate = hits / (hits + misses). Aim for 90%+ in production.
